topics.title: Rainbow Snake surprise with Mortal Kombat friendship banner
topics.trigger: The user wanted a rare, delightful easter egg where roughly one in every hundred games transforms the snake into an animated rainbow and announces it with a celebratory banner. The feature needed to feel visually exciting without affecting normal gameplay at all.
topics.response:
- Added a per-game random roll in `src/main.ts` (`startNewGame`) that activates rainbow mode with a 1-in-100 probability. A `?rainbow=1` or `?rainbow=0` URL parameter overrides the roll deterministically for testing. The rainbow flag is stored as a private field on the game class and held for the entire game session, with the banner hidden whenever the player navigates away from the game screen via `showScreen`.
- Implemented per-segment rainbow coloring in the `draw()` method of `src/main.ts`. Each player-1 snake segment is assigned an HSL hue derived from its position along the body, and the hue offset scrolls over time using `performance.now()`, producing an animated shimmer. The existing per-frame canvas redraw (driven by the board auto-rotation loop) animates the effect for free. Player 2's snake is unaffected in two-player modes.
- Built the Mortal Kombat "FRIENDSHIP!"-style banner in `index.html`, `src/style.css`, and `src/main.ts`. The banner consists of a CSS radial-gradient rainbow arc behind chunky all-caps arcade text that pops in with a bounce keyframe animation, flashes three times, then auto-dismisses via a JavaScript timer after three seconds with a CSS opacity fade.
topics.decisions:
- **Animated shimmer over static gradient**: The rainbow hue was made to scroll continuously along the snake body rather than being a fixed head-to-tail gradient. This keeps the effect visually alive throughout the game and matches the project's established preference for visible, gameplay-paced motion. A static gradient would have been simpler but would have felt inert after the first few seconds.
- **Mortal Kombat "FRIENDSHIP!" aesthetic for the banner**: Rather than a plain colored text overlay (the minimal interpretation of the ticket), the banner was styled as the classic MK friendship finisher — a rainbow arc, chunky arcade lettering, a bounce-in animation, and a flash effect. The deliberately wholesome, over-the-top tone amplifies the surprise and makes the rare event feel genuinely special rather than just informational.
- **URL override seam for testing**: Because a 1-in-100 random event cannot be reliably exercised in automated tests, a `?rainbow=1/0` query parameter was added to force the outcome. This avoids flaky tests and lets manual verification happen instantly without waiting on probability, at the cost of a small amount of URL-parsing code that has no effect in production.
topics.todo: An optional Mortal Kombat-style announcer sound sting was discussed but left out. There is no audio system in the project yet; adding a short WebAudio-generated jingle was noted as an easy future addition if desired.

recap:
The snake game gained a rare easter egg: on roughly one in every hundred games, the player's snake renders as a full animated rainbow that shifts and scrolls along the body throughout the entire session. The effect is exclusive to the player's own snake, so two-player games remain visually distinct with the second player's snake staying its usual blue.

When a rainbow game triggers, a Mortal Kombat "FRIENDSHIP!" inspired banner appears over the board. A semicircular rainbow arc sits behind chunky all-caps arcade lettering that bounces into view, flashes three times, and then fades away automatically after about three seconds — leaving the board unobstructed for the rest of the game. The banner never reappears mid-game and clears itself immediately if the player exits to the menu or dies quickly.
